skill.name: color-font-skill
skill.description: Choose presentation-ready color palettes and font pairings for PPT and design tasks, including brand-safe themes, token colors, and system font guidance.
skill.summary: Select practical presentation color palettes, design tokens, and font pairings for polished visual work.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, C2, and weak cryptography indicators, but manual review found only Markdown tables of hex colors and font guidance. No executable code, network access, shell execution, cryptography, credential handling, or prompt injection text was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backticked Hex Color Values
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The referenced backticks format hex color values in Markdown tables, and there is no Ruby, shell, or executable command context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown palette rows containing color values and usage notes. No code block, interpreter directive, command invocation, or user input execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: C2 Keyword Match in Color Data
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword findings are false positives caused by hexadecimal color strings and table content. The skill contains no network endpoints or command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are inside design palette tables. There is no URL, host, socket, fetch call, beaconing instruction, or remote-control logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives. The referenced Markdown lines describe the skill and a design system, not cryptographic algorithms or security code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, signing, random generation, or authentication implementation. The file is a static design reference.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time selecting colors and fonts for presentation themes. This skill provides curated palettes, token colors, and font guidance for PPT and design work.
actual_capabilities: Recommends 18 named presentation color palettes with usage contexts.
Provides hex color values for white, gold, blue, gray, and opacity scales.
Suggests font choices for Chinese and English presentation content.
Maps palette styles to business, education, healthcare, fashion, and technology scenarios.
Gives practical notes for emphasis colors, backgrounds, and contrast choices.
limitations: It does not generate slides or edit PPT files directly.
It does not test accessibility contrast automatically.
It does not include custom brand asset extraction.
It provides static guidance, not live design trend research.

use_cases.title: Build a Business Presentation Theme
use_cases.description: Select a polished palette and font pairing for reports, pitch decks, and executive presentations.

use_cases.title: Create Consistent Educational Slides
use_cases.description: Choose readable colors and system fonts for training decks, classroom materials, and charts.

use_cases.title: Align Marketing Visuals
use_cases.description: Pick a visual style that matches product, lifestyle, fashion, or food campaign materials.

prompt_templates.title: Pick a Simple Palette
prompt_templates.prompt: Choose a color palette and font pairing for a 10-slide business update. Keep it professional and easy to read.

prompt_templates.title: Match a Topic and Mood
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend colors and fonts for a healthcare presentation with a calm, trustworthy mood. Include background, title, body, and accent guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Chart-Friendly Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a PPT theme for a market analysis deck. Prioritize chart clarity, accessible contrast, and consistent emphasis colors.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt a Design System
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the platinum white-gold design system to define slide backgrounds, text hierarchy, primary actions, dividers, overlays, and highlight colors.

output_examples.input: I need a finance deck that feels serious and premium.
output_examples.output: Use the business authority palette with dark blue titles, light neutral backgrounds, and red only for key data emphasis. Pair Microsoft YaHei with Arial for reliable cross-platform rendering.

output_examples.input: Make a theme for an education report with many charts.
output_examples.output: Use the education and chart palette. Apply deep teal for headings, warm yellow and orange for chart series, and restrained red for warnings.

output_examples.input: Suggest colors for a luxury product launch.
output_examples.output: Use platinum white-gold styling with white surfaces, black text, gold accents, and blue actions. Keep whitespace generous and avoid heavy decorative effects.

best_practices: Start with the presentation topic, audience, and desired mood before choosing colors.
Use one primary color, one accent color, and neutral backgrounds for most slides.
Keep system fonts when the deck must work across different computers.
anti_patterns: Do not combine many palettes in one presentation.
Do not use pale text on pale backgrounds without checking readability.
Do not use decorative fonts for dense body copy or data labels.

faq.question: Can this skill create a PowerPoint file?
faq.answer: No. It provides color and font guidance that can be applied in presentation tools.

faq.question: Does it support Chinese and English typography?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends Microsoft YaHei for Chinese and common system fonts for English.

faq.question: Can I use it for brand-safe themes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help choose restrained palettes and consistent token colors for brand-aligned slides.

faq.question: Does it verify accessibility contrast?
faq.answer: No. You should still test contrast for important text, charts, and action elements.

faq.question: Is this useful outside presentations?
faq.answer: Yes. The palettes and token scales can guide web mockups, reports, and marketing visuals.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a static Markdown reference and does not call external services.
